rulings.Case Title: 3401 CLAY STREET JOINT VENTURE, VS. TIMOTHY BAKA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Yuk May Dora Tang
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2023 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF YUK MAY DORA TANG is transferred to department 301 to be heard at 9:00 a.m. on March 27, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANGELEA ROMBEIRO VS. JESSICA WRIGHT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 14, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT No later than 9:00 a.m, on March 14, 2023 counsel for Plaintiff to file a declaration addressing participation/failure to participate in the meet and confer process. The Court will consider issuing an OSC Re: Contempt for failure to comply with CCP requirements based on the declaration received. Demurrer is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action against the moving defendants and/or to allege facts in support of Plaintiff's allegation that the moving defendants were "acting in concert" with co-defendants. Conclusory allegations against "Defendants" coupled with conclusory allegation that the moving defendants were "acting in concert" with co-defendant are insufficient. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
